title: Centralized Reward Agent for Knowledge Sharing and Transfer in Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning

repos.name: mahaozhe__CenRA
repos.evidence: Repo owner 'mahaozhe' = Haozhe Ma (first author). README title: 'Knowledge Sharing and Transfer via Centralized Reward Agent for Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning'. Paper line 97 states 'The source code is accessible at: https://github.com/mahaozhe/CenRA'. Repo contains CenRA/Algorithms.py (CenRA_dis/CenRA_con classes), CenRA/Agents.py (DQNAgent/SACAgent), CenRA/Networks.py, and run-2dmaze.py/run-3dpickup.py/run-mujococar.py — directly implementing the paper's CenRA framework for the three non-Meta-World environments reported in the paper.

rationale: The single cloned repo mahaozhe__CenRA is unambiguously the paper's own implementation: the repo owner matches the first author, the paper provides the GitHub URL explicitly, and the README and code structure (CenRA_dis/CenRA_con algorithms, centralized reward agent + distributed policy agents) directly implement the paper's CenRA framework. The audit correctly identified this as the paper's code and wrote legitimate 'missing code' findings against real gaps (Meta-World benchmark absent, no eval script, no baseline code, no transfer driver, alpha hard-coded), plus one confirmed runtime bug. No misclassification occurred.
audit_impact: None — the audit correctly used the author's own core repo and all findings are written against the right codebase. The missing-code findings (metaworld-benchmark-absent, eval-script-missing, baselines-not-implemented, knowledge-transfer-driver-missing, alpha-ablation-not-controllable) accurately reflect genuine incompleteness in the released repo relative to the paper's results.
recommended_action: No corrective action needed on the audit classification. The paper is correctly counted as source-present (partial). The legitimate findings should be forwarded to authors as-is.
